title: How Tinnitus Survivors Describe Their Transformation
phrases: The space between knowing something intellectually and knowing it in your body is where all the real work happens.
Embodiment is not a technique. It's what happens when you stop living exclusively in your head.
The self you're trying to improve is the same self doing the improving. Notice the circularity.

title: The Relationship Between Meaning and Suffering in Tinnitus
phrases: What we call 'stuck' is usually the body doing exactly what it was designed to do under conditions that no longer exist.
Not every insight requires action. Some just need to be witnessed.
If your spiritual practice makes you more rigid, it's not working.

title: How Purpose Reduces Tinnitus Impact on Quality of Life
phrases: The gap between stimulus and response is where your entire life lives.
Every resistance is information. The question is whether you're willing to read it.
Every moment of genuine attention is a small act of liberation.
Attention is the most undervalued resource you have. Everything else follows from where you place it.

title: The Practice of Allowing Sound Without Adding Story
phrases: The nervous system doesn't respond to what you believe. It responds to what it senses.
The most sophisticated defense mechanism is the one that looks like wisdom.
The paradox of acceptance is that nothing changes until you stop demanding that it does.

title: How to Distinguish Between Tinnitus and Your Reaction to It
phrases: If your spiritual practice makes you more rigid, it's not working.

The nervous system doesn't respond to what you believe. It responds to what it senses.
The most sophisticated defense mechanism is the one that looks like wisdom.
The contemplative traditions all point to the same thing: what you're looking for is what's looking.

title: The Space Between the Ringing and Your Response
phrases: The mind is not the enemy. The identification with it is.

The body has a grammar. Most of us never learned to read it.
There's a meaningful difference between self-improvement and self-understanding. One adds. The other reveals.

title: The Practice of Deep Listening Beyond Tinnitus
phrases: Most of what passes for healing is just rearranging the furniture in a burning house.
Trauma reorganizes perception. Recovery reorganizes it again, but this time with your participation.
The body remembers what the mind would prefer to file away.
The research is clear on this, and it contradicts almost everything popular culture teaches.

title: How Tinnitus Opens the Door to Embodied Awareness
phrases: Complexity is the ego's favorite hiding place.

title: The Relationship Between Stillness and Sound
